reforms.description: The Finnish government aims to strengthen the interaction between science and industry to bolster R&D activity in Finland, which has already reached nearly 3% of GDP, the second highest in the EU. Additionally, it intends to use proceeds from privatisation to maintain the current level of expenditure for research and development and to provide venture capital and additional resources for universities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening R&D interaction and maintaining funding levels are expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced its intentions to strengthen R&D funding and interaction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but do not represent a complete overhaul of the R&D funding system.
reforms.source_quote: Privatisation proceeds will be mainly used to repay state debt, with some of the proceeds used to maintain the current level of expenditure for research and development and some to provide venture capital and additional resources for universities.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has been channeling public R&D support primarily through research councils of the Academy of Finland and technology development centres (TEKES), which aim to enhance the transfer of innovations and new technology from universities and public research centres to companies.
reforms.component_name: Public R&D Support for Knowledge Transfer
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the transfer of innovations from research to industry is expected to stimulate economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has successfully implemented the channeling of public R&D support through designated councils and centres.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the mechanisms for knowledge transfer but does not fundamentally change the overall system.
reforms.source_quote: Public R&D support is mainly channelled through research councils of the Academy of Finland and through technology development centres (TEKES) of the Ministry of Trade and Industry.
